title: Trump’s Complaint About Israeli Strike on Gas Field Exposes Divergent Strategies
summary: President Trump said he told Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel he disapproved of the attack, which sent energy markets reeling. But Israeli officials said the Americans were informed beforehand.

title: Trump Says He Won’t Send Troops to Iran But Leaves Wiggle Room
summary: The president was cagey about his plans for Iran. He confirmed the Pentagon was seeking $200 billion to support a protracted war effort while also claiming it would be over soon.

title: Trump Jokes About Pearl Harbor in Meeting With Japan’s Leader
summary: Breaking a taboo, President Trump needled Japan’s prime minister about the World War II attack, as she widened her eyes and appeared to take a deep breath in the Oval Office.

title: Trump’s Handpicked Arts Commission Approves Gold Coin With His Face on It
summary: Many of America’s founders were fiercely against taking steps that would make its government officials appear like kings, and that included featuring them on the country’s coins.

title: Cesar Chavez Avenue May Soon Be Gone. Yet to Be Confronted: His Legacy.
summary: After the revelations of sex abuse, the public is left to make sense of the labor leader’s work and life.

title: ‘We’re Just Seen as Sex Objects’: Dolores Huerta’s Years in the U.F.W.
summary: The co-founder of the United Farm Workers talked about her relationship with Cesar Chavez, and the night he raped her.

title: Cesar Chavez, a Civil Rights Icon, Is Accused of Abusing Girls for Years
summary: An investigation by The New York Times found extensive evidence that the United Farm Workers co-founder groomed and sexually abused girls who worked in the movement.

title: A Gift From Trump to the Supreme Court
summary: In a caustic critique of the court issued on social media late Sunday night, the president inadvertently buttressed its independence.

title: How Did Flea Make a Jazz Album? Practice, Practice, Practice.
summary: The Red Hot Chili Peppers bassist returned to the trumpet, for a new record featuring Nick Cave, Thom Yorke and a core cast of contemporary jazz luminaries.

title: I Taught My Son Everything, Except How to Take a Vacation
summary: He was about to leave for college when I realized: I had never taken him on a real vacation.

title: What to Know About State Laws on Ten Commandments Displays in Classrooms
summary: Texas, Louisiana and Arkansas have passed laws requiring the posters in public schools. Several other states are considering similar measures.

title: ‘Bachelorette’ Season With Taylor Frankie Paul Canceled After Assault Video
summary: Taylor Frankie Paul, who was set to star in the TV show, had pleaded guilty to aggravated assault after an encounter with her partner in 2023.

title: Horror Novel ‘Shy Girl’ Canceled Over Suspected A.I. Use
summary: Its publisher, Hachette, will not release the novel in the United States and will discontinue its U.K. edition, citing its commitment to “original creative expression and storytelling.”

title: Jeff Bezos in Talks to Raise $100 Billion Fund to Transform Companies With A.I.
summary: The new fund would operate alongside the Amazon founder’s A.I. start-up, Project Prometheus.

title: The Long Farewell to Mark Zuckerberg’s Metaverse
summary: Meta announced changes that effectively leave Mr. Zuckerberg’s vision of an immersive digital world based in virtual reality only on life support.

title: She Killed a Family With Her Speeding Car. Is Probation Enough?
summary: Two years ago, an older driver killed a couple, their toddler and their baby as her vehicle sped through San Francisco. A judge has indicated that he intends to let her avoid prison, home detention and community service.

title: New Museum Debuts New Building With Ambitious ‘New Humans’
summary: It’s a big, serious, adult show worth debating and even fighting over — just the way our critic likes it.

title: Can Military Excellence Save Us From Trump’s Incompetence?
summary: It should never have come to this.

title: Trump Is Putting His Stamp on the World
summary: Be careful what you wish for.

title: White House Seeks to Reassure Americans on Rising Costs
summary: Also, March Madness begins. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: Nashville Reporter Released From ICE Custody
summary: Estefany Maria Rodriguez Florez, who works for a Spanish-language outlet, was released on bond in a case that has raised concerns about press freedom.

title: Is Russian Fuel Headed for Cuba, Testing the U.S. Blockade?
summary: A Russian oil tanker is being closely tracked to see if it will challenge the Trump administration’s blockade on Cuba.

title: Monopoly? Not a Chance, Says Live Nation’s Chief at Antitrust Trial.
summary: Michael Rapino, the chief executive of the live entertainment colossus, fought back against accusations his company unfairly dominates the music industry.

title: Body of Missing University of Alabama Student Is Found Dead in Spain, Police Say
summary: James Gracey, 20, a student at the University of Alabama, disappeared during a visit to a beachfront nightclub in Barcelona. His body was found on Thursday, the police said.
